subject: math
prompt: A machine fills bags with approximately 32 ounces of almonds. To test the accuracy of the filling process, 144 bags of almonds were selected at random and weighed. Based on the sample, it is estimated that the average weight of all bags of almonds filled by the machine in a 10-hour period is 31.86 ounces, with an associated margin of error of 0.18 ounce. Which of the following is the best interpretation of this estimate?
questionText: A machine fills bags with approximately 32 ounces of almonds. To test the accuracy of the filling process, 144 bags of almonds were selected at random and weighed. Based on the sample, it is estimated that the average weight of all bags of almonds filled by the machine in a 10-hour period is 31.86 ounces, with an associated margin of error of 0.18 ounce. Which of the following is the best interpretation of this estimate?

choices.text: Plausible values for the average weight of all bags of almonds filled by the machine are between 31.68 ounces and 32.04 ounces.
Plausible values for the average weight of all bags of almonds filled by the machine are less than 31.68 ounces or greater than 32.04 ounces.
The average weight of all bags of almonds filled by the machine is less than 31.99 ounces.
The average weight of all bags of almonds filled by the machine is greater than 31.99 ounces.

subject: math
prompt: The function $f(x) = 250(1.035)^x$ models the value, in dollars, of a certain bank account from 1954 through 1969, where $x$ is the number of years after the end of 1954. Which of the following is the best interpretation of "$f(5)$ is approximately equal to 297" in this context?
questionText: The function $f(x) = 250(1.035)^x$ models the value, in dollars, of a certain bank account from 1954 through 1969, where $x$ is the number of years after the end of 1954. Which of the following is the best interpretation of "$f(5)$ is approximately equal to 297" in this context?

choices.text: The value of the bank account is estimated to be approximately 5 dollars greater at the end of 1959 than at the end of 1954.
The value of the bank account is estimated to be approximately 297 dollars at the end of 1959.
The value, in dollars, of the bank account is estimated to be approximately 5 times greater at the end of 1959 than at the end of 1954.
The value of the bank account is estimated to increase by approximately 297 dollars every 5 years between the end of 1954 and the end of 1969.

choices.text: The mean age for the new collection is greater than the mean age for the original collection, and the median age for the new collection is greater than the median age for the original collection.
The mean age for the new collection is greater than the mean age for the original collection, but the median age is the same for the original and new collections.

prompt: A researcher is designing a study to investigate the average number of minutes students at a high school spend exercising per day. The researcher will report an estimated average number of minutes students at the high school spend exercising per day with an associated margin of error. The researcher is considering using a random sample of either 120 or 240 students from the high school. Which of the following would be the most likely effect of using the larger random sample compared to the smaller random sample?
questionText: A researcher is designing a study to investigate the average number of minutes students at a high school spend exercising per day. The researcher will report an estimated average number of minutes students at the high school spend exercising per day with an associated margin of error. The researcher is considering using a random sample of either 120 or 240 students from the high school. Which of the following would be the most likely effect of using the larger random sample compared to the smaller random sample?

choices.text: The reported margin of error would be lower.
The reported margin of error would be higher.
The reported average number of minutes would be lower.
The reported average number of minutes would be higher.

subject: math
prompt: The frequency table summarizes a data set of the weights, rounded to the nearest pound, of 71 Ardeotis kori birds. A weight of 32 pounds is added to the original data set, creating a new data set of the weights, rounded to the nearest pound, of 72 Ardeotis kori birds. Which statement best compares the mean and median of the new data set to the mean and median of the original data set?
questionText: The frequency table summarizes a data set of the weights, rounded to the nearest pound, of 71 Ardeotis kori birds. A weight of 32 pounds is added to the original data set, creating a new data set of the weights, rounded to the nearest pound, of 72 Ardeotis kori birds. Which statement best compares the mean and median of the new data set to the mean and median of the original data set?

choices.text: The mean of the new data set is greater than the mean of the original data set, and the median of the new data set is greater than the median of the original data set.
The mean of the new data set is greater than the mean of the original data set, and the medians of the two data sets are equal.

subject: math
prompt: In the given equation, $ x(7x - 2a) + b(7x - 2a) - (x + b) = 0 $, $ a $ and $ b $ are positive constants. A solution to the equation is $ x = 36 $. What is the value of $ a $?

prompt:

prompt:

prompt: For groups of 25 or more people, a museum charges \$22 per person for the first 25 people and \$14 for each additional person. Which function $f$ gives the total charge, in dollars, for a tour group with $n$ people, where $n \geq 25$?
